page.content: This report presents an analysis of the efficacy of two distinct approaches to HIV counselling and testing among participants in the study. The table below compares the outcomes of Abbreviated HIV Counselling and Testing (AHT) with those of Traditional HIV Counselling and Testing (THT). A key focus of this analysis is to determine the relative effectiveness of these methodologies in reaching and engaging individuals at risk for HIV. The data highlights the performance metrics associated with each approach, specifically examining the percentage of individuals who accepted testing and subsequently received results.

page.content: In addition to the testing acceptance rates, the table includes a critical metric: the Difference (95% one-sided upper CI), which provides insight into the statistical significance of the observed variations between AHT and THT. Understanding these differences is crucial for informing public health strategies and optimizing resource allocation in HIV prevention efforts. By evaluating both counselling methods, this report aims to contribute to the ongoing discourse on best practices in HIV testing, ultimately enhancing intervention strategies and improving health outcomes for targeted populations. The findings underscore the importance of adopting evidence-based approaches to effectively combat the HIV epidemic.
